document.title: StickerDB Execution Satellite Job Cell
document.purpose: Describe the requester-owned job cell used by StickerDB when preparing execution satellite jobs.
author: Lion Kimbro
Wing-Cat

summary.one-sentence: A job cell is a requester-owned folder containing the request, input argument files, response data files, and response control file for one execution satellite job.
summary.plain-language: StickerDB creates a little folder-world for each satellite job. The satellite may read from it and write to requested paths inside it, but StickerDB owns the folder and cleans it up later.

root.description: The requester-owned folder for one job.

typical-files.request.json: The request written by StickerDB and read by the satellite.
typical-files.response.json: The response written by the satellite to the request's response_path.

request-args.description: Requester-created files that serve as arguments to the job.

response-data.description: Files the satellite is asked to produce for the requester.

requester-owns: Cleanup of the entire job cell.
satellite-may-do: Read files named in the request input section.
Write files named in the request output section.
Write jobctrl/response.json when response_path points there.
Keep separate satellite-owned local records, copies, logs, or cache.
satellite-must-not-do: Rename or move requester-owned files unless the request explicitly asks for that behavior.
Assume requester-owned files are available after expires_at.

steps.name: create-job-id
steps.description: StickerDB creates a job identifier such as job-ed3fa723.

steps.name: create-job-cell
steps.description: StickerDB creates the job cell folder and standard subfolders.

steps.name: write-request-args
steps.description: StickerDB writes input files into request-args or otherwise prepares full input paths.

steps.name: write-request-json
steps.description: StickerDB writes jobctrl/request.json with schema, job_id, job, created_at, expires_at, input, output, and response_path.

steps.name: submit-to-satellite
steps.description: StickerDB makes the request visible to the satellite, typically by copying the request or writing a pointer into the satellite inbox.

steps.name: wait-for-response
steps.description: StickerDB watches or later checks response_path.

steps.name: consume-response
steps.description: StickerDB reads response.json and imports any produced response-data files if appropriate.

steps.name: cleanup
steps.description: StickerDB eventually deletes or archives the job cell according to its own policy.

steps.name: receive-request
steps.description: The satellite receives a copy of the request or a pointer to the request.

steps.name: check-expiration
steps.description: If the current time is after expires_at, the satellite may drop or ignore the job from its own side.

steps.name: show-pending-job
steps.description: The satellite displays the job to the operator as pending work.

steps.name: execute-when-operator-starts
steps.description: When the operator starts execution from the prepared environment, the satellite performs the job.

steps.name: write-response
steps.description: The satellite writes response JSON to response_path.

steps.name: clean-own-records
steps.description: The satellite may clean its own inbox entries, copies, logs, and cache according to its own rules.

submission-model.description: The job cell is owned by StickerDB. The satellite may still receive a local inbox record so it knows the request exists.

acceptable-v1-approaches.name: copy-request-json
acceptable-v1-approaches.description: StickerDB copies jobctrl/request.json into the satellite inbox. The copied request still contains full paths back to the requester-owned job cell.

acceptable-v1-approaches.name: pointer-file
acceptable-v1-approaches.description: StickerDB writes a small file into the satellite inbox containing the full path to jobctrl/request.json.

submission-model.reason: A copied request is simple, self-contained, and still uses full paths to requester-owned files.

expiration.meaning-for-satellite: After this time, the satellite may delete or ignore its own local record of the job without asking StickerDB.
expiration.meaning-for-requester: StickerDB may decide whether to re-up, recreate, archive, or delete the requester-owned job cell.
expiration.important-distinction: Expiration allows the satellite to clean its own side. It does not give the satellite ownership of the requester job cell.

design-notes: The job cell is meant to be legible when inspected manually.
request-args means files supplied as arguments to the request.
response-data means files produced as data for the requester.
jobctrl means control-plane files such as request and response JSON.
The satellite should not need to infer where response files belong; the request tells it response_path.
The requester may use the job cell as a temporary communication cell and delete it later.
